identity.name: @epdoc/transform
identity.purpose: Deep copy, JSON serialization/deserialization and string replacement

quickStart.pattern: import { Deep, Json } → Deep.copy() / Json.serialize() / Json.deserialize()

exports.name: Deep.copy
exports.description: Deep copy with optional string replacement

exports.name: Deep.hasSimpleReplacements
exports.description: Type guard for string-only replacement config

exports.name: Deep.hasComplexReplacements
exports.description: Type guard for advanced replacement config

exports.name: Deep.processStringWithReplacements
exports.description: Core string substitution used by serialize/deserialize

exports.name: Json.serialize
exports.whenToUse: When you need to preserve Set/Map/RegExp/Uint8Array in JSON

patterns.name: Basic deep copy
patterns.when: Clone an object while substituting template vars

patterns.name: Advanced deep copy with dates
patterns.code: Deep.copy(obj, { replace: { now: new Date() }, msubFn: (s, r) => replace(s, r) })

patterns.name: Serialize/deserialize special types
patterns.when: Round-trip Set, Map, RegExp, Uint8Array through JSON
patterns.code:
const json = Json.serialize(data, { encode: true });
const data2 = Json.deserialize(json, { decode: true });

patterns.name: Serialize/deserialize Temporal
patterns.code:
const json = Json.serialize({ t: Temporal.Instant.from('2024-01-15T12:00:00Z') });
const obj = Json.deserialize(json, { decode: true });

patterns.name: Auto-detect RegExp in plain objects
patterns.when: Restore { regex, flags } shapes to RegExp during deep copy or deserialize
patterns.code: Deep.copy({ re: { regex: '^foo', flags: 'i' } }, { autoRegExp: true })
